title: Celtics' Chris Cenac Jr. Tells Fans What To Expect From Him
description: The Boston Celtics selected Chris Cenac Jr. from the University of Houston with the 27th overall pick in the NBA Draft on Tuesday night.
fullText:
Celtics' Chris Cenac Jr. Tells Fans What To Expect From Him originally appeared on NESN. 

The Boston Celtics selected Chris Cenac Jr. from the University of Houston with the 27th overall pick in the NBA Draft on Tuesday night.

Cenac, a 6-foot-11, 240-pound forward/center, will give Brad Stevens the help he was searching for in the frontcourt. After the season ended, Stevens noted that he wants to improve the impact the team has at the rim. He averaged 9.5 points and 7.6 rebounds per game last season, and told Celtics fans what they should expect from him.

"They’re going to get a great character guy who’s going to come in and work hard every day and do whatever it takes to help the team win,” he told ESPN’s Lisa Salters moments after being selected, per Taylor Snow.

The 19-year-old played just one season of college basketball and was a huge reason Houston was a two seed in the NCAA Tournament. He scored 17 points and logged nine rebounds in Houston's Round of 32 matchup with Texas A&M. In the Round of 64, he had an 18 rebound game against Idaho.

Houston's season ended in the Sweet 16 against Illinois. Cenac had 10 rebounds in the game, and even though the season was over, he established himself as a first-round talent.

title: Nate Ament taken by the Milwaukee Bucks with the 13th pick of the 2026 NBA Draft
description: Nate Ament ended up where most experts figured he would — even if it wasn’t exactly at the moment they predicted. Ament was selected by the Milwaukee Bucks with pick No. 13 in the 2026 NBA Draft on Tuesday night at the Barclays Center. Well, technically, it was the Miami Heat who made the pick […]
fullText:
Nate Ament ended up where most experts figured he would — even if it wasn’t exactly at the moment they predicted.

Ament was selected by the Milwaukee Bucks with pick No. 13 in the 2026 NBA Draft on Tuesday night at the Barclays Center.

Nate Ament is selected 13th overall by the @MiamiHEAT in the 2026 NBA Draft!

Watch on ABC & ESPN. pic.twitter.com/8LuWumorQn

Well, technically, it was the Miami Heat who made the pick because they held the pick, but it was included as part of the blockbuster trade between Miami and the Bucks that saw superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo heading to South Florida in exchange for a major haul. Adam Silver later announced the trade of the pick to the Bucks.

Most experts had projected Ament to go at No. 10 to Milwaukee, but the Bucks tabbed Arizona guard Brayden Burries there instead.

Ament is heading to a Bucks team that now will be in the middle of a full rebuild, and as a first round pick and with Antetokounmpo leaving town, opportunity abounds for Ament to carve out a role and serve as a piece to try and turn around Jimmy Haslam’s NBA franchise.

Best of luck in Milwaukee, Nate.

title: NBA Draft Tracker 2026: Full Results, List of Picks From Rounds 1-2
description: Follow along right here for the full results from the 2026 NBA Draft.
fullText:
NBA Draft Tracker 2026: Full Results, List of Picks From Rounds 1-2 originally appeared on NESN. 

The Wizards are officially on the clock.

On Tuesday night, the 2026 NBA Draft will begin when Washington selects a potential superstar with the No. 1 overall pick. The Wizards could take BYU's AJ Dybantsa, Kansas' Darryn Peterson or Duke's Cameron Boozer.

Beyond the top prospects, there is plenty of depth in this draft class, so contending teams like the Celtics could find steals late in the first round and second round.

The 2026 NBA Draft has arrived. The first round will begin and conclude on June 23, with the second round to follow on June 24. Duke is among the teams expected to hear multiple names called in the 2026 NBA Draft.

This will track the draft news, rumors and eventual landing spots of the Draft eligible Duke Blue Devils. Keeping you up to date on any draft related movement as it pertains to any Duke Draft news.

Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green recently previewed the NBA Draft on his podcast, "The Draymond Green Show," and in doing so spent some time evaluating former Duke star Cameron Boozer. When it got to Boozer, Green mentioned him alongside one of the greatest power forwards in NBA history. "Tim Duncan never had to blow you through the roof. Yet he was so great. He's one of the greatest players we've ever seen," said Green.

There is a chance either Washington or Utah will call Boozer’s name before Peterson. Why? "Availability is your best ability,” Farnham said on First Take. “There’s nothing that I looked at over the course of the season at Kansas that would say, ‘Okay, I believe he can make it through 82 games.’ Jerry West said, ‘If you can’t play 30, how can you play 82?’ I think that’s the concern for Darryn Peterson. If Darryn Peterson didn’t have any health issues and was available all season long and had that quick-twitch that we saw him have at the high school level, then yes, I would agree that he is an elite-level prospect that can get his shot off at any spot,” the ESPN analyst added.
